name: Chinese Pineapple Chicken (Bow Luo Gai)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------MARINADE-------------------------
1/2 tsp Salt
1/2 tsp Sugar
1 tbsp Thin Soy Sauce
Dash of Pepper
1 Thin Slice Ginger, Chopped
--------------------------BATTER--------------------------
1 Large Egg
1 Tablespoon Water
2 1/2 Tablespoons Flour
3 Tablespoons Cornstarch
1. Remove skin and bones from chicken. Cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Sprinkle chicken with each of the ingredients listed under "marinade," mix well and marinate for 30 minutes.
3. Cut celery into 1 1/2-inch pieces; then, cut each piece lengthwise into strips (julienne style).
4. Drain pineapple, saving the juice.
5. Prepare batter by beating the egg, adding the water, flour, and cornstarch. Mix thoroughly.
6. Heat oil to 350 degrees in a small saucepan.
7. Dip chicken in batter and drop into the hot oil. Deep-fry for 10 minutes or until golden brown. Remove and drain excess oil on paper towels.
8. In a wok, combine pineapple juice, water, catsup, vinegar, sugar, and dash of salt. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
9. Add celery, chicken, and pineapple to the saucepan. Cook for 1 minute over high heat until heated through.
10. Add thickening made by combining cornstarch and cold water. Mix well and cook for an additional minute before serving.
